popularStory.headline: King Robert the Bruce
popularStory.description:
Robert the Bruce was born in 1274, probably at Turnberry, Ayrshire. He was the eldest son of Robert Bruce, a competitor in the succession case that followed the death of King Alexander III.

A succe …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots於1274年7月11日出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾騰恩貝利時，他的父親Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale31歲，母親Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick20歲。他於1290年與Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他於1306年登記服兵役。他於1306年被列為king of scotland。他去世於1329年6月7日在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾卡德洛斯享年54歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林登福姆林 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots于1274年7月11日出生在苏格兰艾斯爾騰恩貝利时，他的父亲Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale31岁，母亲Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick20岁。他于1290年与Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他于1306年登记服兵役。他于1306年被列为king of scotland。他去世于1329年6月7日在苏格兰登巴頓史爾卡德洛斯享年54岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰費伏登福姆林登福姆林 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Scots
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly an altered form of Dutch Soodts, of uncertain origin.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a variant spelling of Ty or a transferred use of the surname, a local name for someone who lived by a common pasture, Middle English tye.

nameMeanings.name: Bruce,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the Scottish surname, now used as a given name throughout the English-speaking world. In the 20th century it was particularly popular in Australia. The surname was originally a Norman baronial name, but a precise identification of the place from which it was derived has not been made (there are a large number of possible candidates). The Bruces were an influential Norman family in Scottish affairs in the early Middle Ages; its most famous member was  Robert  ‘the Bruce’ (  1274–1329 ), who is said to have drawn inspiration after his defeat at Methven from the perseverance of a spider in repeatedly climbing up again after being knocked down. He ruled Scotland as  King Robert I  from  1306 to 1329 .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots was born on 11 July 1274, in Turnberry, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale, was 31 and his mother, Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, was 20. He married Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick in 1290. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1306. In 1306, his occupation is listed as king of scotland. He died on 7 June 1329, in Cardross, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, at the age of 54, and was buried in Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots est né le 11 juillet 1274, à Turnberry, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick en 1290. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1306. En 1306, sa profession de son indiquée est king of scotland. Il est décédé le 7 juin 1329, à Cardross, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots am 11. Juli 1274 in Turnberry, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale 31 und seine Mutter Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick 20. Er hat 1290 Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1306 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1306 ist sein Beruf als king of scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 7. Juni 1329 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Cardross, Dunbartonshire, Scotland und wurde in Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert I the Bruce, King of Scotsが1274年7月11日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアターンバーリーで生まれた時，父Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandaleは31歳，母Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrickは20歳であった。1290年にIsabella of Mar Countess of Carrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1306年に兵役登録をした。1306年，職業はking of scotlandと記録されている。彼は1329年6月7日にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアカードロスで54歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインダンフエルムライン アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.Bridge of Awe, Argyll, Scotland, United: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 브리지 프 아베

ko.lifeSummary: Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots(이)가 1274년 7월 11일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 터른베리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1290년 Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1306년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1306년, 그의 직업은 king of scotland이었다. 그는 1329년 6월 7일 스컷랜드 던바토실르 카드로스에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 피프 던퍼라이인 던퍼라이인 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots nasceu em 11 de julho de 1274, em Turnberry, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick em 1290. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1306. Em 1306, sua profissão aparece como king of scotland. Ele faleceu em 7 de junho de 1329, em Cardross, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Bridge of Awe, Argyll, Scotland, United: бридж оф ав, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fifeshir: дунфирмлаин эбби, дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots родился 11 июля 1274 г. в местности турнбирри, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick в 1290 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. В 1306 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1306 годуработал по профессии king of scotland. Он умер 7 июня 1329 г. в местности кардросс, дунбартоншир, Шотландия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности дунфирмлаин эбби, дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert I the Bruce, King of Scots nació el 11 de julio de 1274, en Turnberry, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Robert de Brus 6th Lord of Annandale, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Marjorie de Carrick 3rd Countess of Carrick, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Isabella of Mar Countess of Carrick en 1290. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1306. En 1306, su ocupación registrada era king of scotland. Murió el 7 de junio de 1329, en Cardross, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Dunfermline Abbey, Dunfermline, Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido.
